desc: Ulcinj is Montenegro's southern beach-and-wind town, known for long sandy shoreline, Ottoman-influenced old-town culture, and strong kitesurf seasonality. Compared with Kotor/Budva, it feels more spacious and value-focused, with biggest demand in midsummer and better room-to-price balance in shoulder months.

todo.name: Ulcinj Old Town (Stari Grad)
todo.desc: Explore fortified lanes, sea-view terraces and small museums above the Adriatic. Best in late afternoon light.

todo.name: Velika Plaža
todo.desc: A vast sandy shoreline ideal for long walks, sunset swims and space even in peak months.

todo.name: Ada Bojana
todo.desc: River-island delta area known for wild beach scenery, seafood restaurants and laid-back atmosphere.

todo.name: Kitesurf lessons
todo.desc: Steady thermal winds and shallow sections make summer one of the Adriatic's best beginner-friendly kitesurf spots.

todo.name: Mala Plaža promenade
todo.desc: Small-town seafront packed with cafes and gelato bars, busiest after sunset in July and August.

todo.name: Salina bird reserve
todo.desc: Watch migratory birds in the restored salt pans, especially productive in spring and autumn shoulder months.

wifi.note: Usable across town and main beachfront areas; speeds can vary in remote beach strips and smaller guesthouses.

prac.fltNote: No direct London departures; return fares are often ~£90–£200 off-peak, ~£200–£330 in shoulder demand, and ~£330+ in peak summer weeks.
prac.visa: Visa-free for UK citizens — up to 90 days in any 180-day period.
bestFor: Families wanting long sandy shallow-water beaches
alerts: Peak-season road traffic can significantly extend airport transfers.
Beach infrastructure and services scale down outside main season.
Wind conditions for kitesurf are best in-season; check daily forecasts and licensed operators.

sweet: June and September are usually the sweet spot for warm water, active beach services, and fewer crowds than July–August.
